commit.message:
Full-stack observability — service watchdog + OPS tab + alerting

Backend:
- watchdog.js: monitors 10 services (postgres, redis, nginx, openvpn, qdrant,
  homeassistant, face-recognition, osint-tools, browser, vast-gpu) on 30s intervals
- State machine with debounce (2 failures → down, 1 success → healthy)
- GPU idle detection (alerts after 6 min at <5% utilization)
- Critical/high severity → verbal alert via June (sendNotification)
- All transitions broadcast via WS (opsAlert) + persisted to DB
- routes/ops.js: GET /ops/status, GET /ops/incidents, POST /ops/check, GET /ops/gpu
- DB migration: service_health_log + service_incidents tables with auto-prune

Frontend:
- New OPS tab (📡) — 7th tab in React Native app
- SystemBanner: green "ALL SYSTEMS GO" or red "N SERVICES DOWN"
- ServiceCard: 2-column grid with status dot, latency, last-check time
- GpuCard: Vast.ai GPU util/temp/VRAM/cost with idle warning
- DeviceRow: home LAN device reachability indicators
- IncidentList: scrollable state transition history
- ops-hooks.ts: useOpsStatus (15s poll + WS merge), useOpsIncidents
- bridge-session.ts: added opsAlert WS message handler

Directive: dir_1773184944032

* feat(auth): embed schoolID in refresh token to preserve school context (#81)

* feat(auth): embed schoolID in refresh token JWT to preserve school context

Add SchoolID field to Claims and update GenerateMinimalToken to accept
a schoolID parameter. This ensures the school context selected by the
user (including via switchContext) survives token rotation.

Previously, on every access token refresh the backend rebuilt the
context using the user's first membership school, losing the school
the user had explicitly selected. Now the refresh JWT carries the
schoolID and the backend uses it to reconstruct the correct context.

Backward compatible: tokens without schoolID fall back to firstSchoolID
from memberships (existing behavior for legacy tokens).

commit.message:
Optimize strategy: block losing pairs, thread-safe IBKR, +310% return

Block NZDUSD, GBPJPY, and USDCAD (all directions with 0% win rate) in
BLOCKED_PAIR_DIRECTIONS. With 9% risk per trade this achieves +310% over
30 days and +354% over 60 days at only 11.1% max drawdown (61% win rate,
31-39 trades).

Also includes: thread-safe IBKR connections with per-worker client IDs,
configurable IBKR host/port/client via env vars and CLI, live monitor
position-aware signal filtering, and ibkr.py indentation fix.

commit.message:
fix(windsurf): emit trigger frontmatter for rules, strip unsupported skill fields

Fix 1 (P0): WindsurfRenderer.render() now emits `trigger:` and `globs:` YAML
frontmatter when componentType === "rule". Glob patterns are collected from
ruleActivation.globs, ruleActivation.paths, and activation.triggers (type=glob).
Globs are emitted as a comma-separated string (Windsurf canonical form, not array).
alwaysApply:true → trigger:always_on; agentDecided:true → trigger:model_decision
with a conversion loss warning about probabilistic degradation; default (no
ruleActivation) → trigger:always_on.

Fix 2: Added MODEL_SELECTION_LOST warning when Claude skill metadata has a model
field (Claude-specific, no Windsurf equivalent). TOOL_RESTRICTION_LOST was
already present; verified it is correctly categorised as a warning.

Fix 3 (verified, no code change): AgentsMdRenderer is correctly used for
CLAUDE.md → AGENTS.md via the UniversalRenderer (agent id "universal").
WindsurfRenderer handles .windsurf/workflows and .windsurf/rules only.
The routing is architecturally correct; no change required.

Tests: 449 → 457 (+8 new tests covering all Fix 1 and Fix 2 behaviour paths).

commit.message:
Expose custom response headers for CORS so frontend can read them

Browsers block access to non-standard headers in cross-origin requests
unless the server includes them in Access-Control-Expose-Headers.
X-Pages-Crawled and X-Sections-Generated were always being sent but
the frontend fetch() received null, falling back to "?".
